# OC Value Pro — Orange County Date-of-Death Appraisals

> OC Value Pro is the Orange County, California date-of-death and estate real estate appraisal practice of Brian Ward, a California Certified Residential Real Estate Appraiser (License No. AR036053), serving the region since 2004 with 20+ years of experience and 7,000+ appraisals. It is a sister site of brianward.com. Desktop appraisals are prepared for the IRS step-up in basis (IRC §1014), Form 706 estate-tax filings, trust administration, and probate. Basic Desktop from $299; Standard Desktop from $449. Reports are USPAP- and IRS-compliant, prepared without a physical inspection, and delivered in 3–5 business days.

## Core Pages
- [Home](https://www.ocvaluepro.com/index.html): Overview of Orange County date-of-death appraisals and the step-up in basis.
- [Why Desktop](https://www.ocvaluepro.com/why-desktop.html): Why the desktop method suits retrospective estate valuation.
- [How It Works](https://www.ocvaluepro.com/how-it-works.html): The order-to-delivery process.
- [Pricing](https://www.ocvaluepro.com/pricing.html): Flat-rate Basic ($299) and Standard ($449) desktop tiers.
- [Step-Up in Basis Guide](https://www.ocvaluepro.com/step-up-in-basis.html): IRC §1014, California double step-up, and why the appraisal is the proof.
- [FAQ](https://www.ocvaluepro.com/faq.html): Common questions on IRS acceptance, retrospective dates, and turnaround.
- [About & Credentials](https://www.ocvaluepro.com/about.html): Appraiser credentials and scope.
- [Service Areas](https://www.ocvaluepro.com/service-areas.html): All Orange County cities and communities served.
- [Order Now](https://www.ocvaluepro.com/order.html): Online order form.
- [Contact](https://www.ocvaluepro.com/contact.html): Contact form.

## Orange County Areas Served

## Notes
- Parent practice: brianward.com (San Diego & Riverside Counties).
- This site does not publish fabricated testimonials.
